chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Allah {swt}, the Almighty, Has Made Permissible and Prohibited in Marriage

text:
4470 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ:
كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ أَنَّ رَجُلاً خَطَبَ إِلَى عَمٍّ لَهُ اِبْنَتَهُ فَأَمَرَ بَعْضَ إِخْوَتِهِ أَنْ يُزَوِّجَهُ اِبْنَتَهُ اَلَّتِي خَطَبَهَا وَ أَنَّ اَلرَّجُلَ أَخْطَأَ بِاسْمِ اَلْجَارِيَةِ وَ كَانَ اِسْمُهَا فَاطِمَةَ فَسَمَّاهَا بِغَيْرِ اِسْمِهَا وَ لَيْسَ لِلرَّجُلِ اِبْنَةٌ بِاسْمِ اَلَّتِي ذَكَرَ اَلْمُزَوِّجُ فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4470 - Muhammad ibn Abd al-Hamid narrated from Muhammad ibn Shuayb who said:
I wrote to Imam (as) regarding a man who proposed marriage to his uncle's daughter.
The uncle instructed one of his brothers to marry his daughter to the man.
However, during the marriage contract, the man mistakenly mentioned a different name instead of the daughter's actual name, which was Fatimah. The man did not have any daughter by the name he mentioned during the contract.
The Imam (as) responded: "There is no harm in it."
